data.title: DOJ says Trump's firing of Fed's Cook wouldn't trigger market turmoil
data.description:
The U.S. Justice Department told the Supreme Court Friday that President Donald Trump ousting Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook would not rattle financial markets or endanger Fed independence.

Solicitor General D. John Sauer urged SCOTUS to let Trump's effort to remove Cook to move forward while her case against him plays out.

"The President suffers irreparable harm from the reinstatement of a Federal Reserve Governor who, in his judgment, lacks the 'competence and trustworthiness' needed to serve as a senior financial regulator," Sauer said in the Supreme Court filing.

"On the other side, recognizing the President’s power to remove Governors for apparent financial misfeasance would not compromise the Federal Reserve’s policy independence, nor would removing Cook on that basis usher financial market disaster," Sauer added.

A bipartisan group of former top economic policymakers on Thursday filed a brief urging the Supreme Court to block U.S. President Donald Trump from firing Fed Governor Lisa Cook, arguing for the independence of the central bank.

Trump in late August attempted to fire Cook, alleging that she had committed mortgage fraud in connection with applications she filed for two residential properties she owns. Cook has denied any wrongdoing.

Cook's counsel has said that Trump's case to remove her is unlikely to succeed on its merits and said his interpretation of "for cause" in purportedly firing her "would destroy the Federal Reserve's historic independence."

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: CAVA Group COO Jennifer Somers departs
data.description:
• CAVA Group's (NYSE:CAVA) COO Jennifer Somers has departed the company.
  • Somers' departure constitutes a Covered Termination without Cause under the company’s Executive Severance Plan. The company will pay Somers the severance benefits as per the plan.
  • The company has initiated a search to identify a successor. In the interim, Jonathan Braatvedt, SVP of Operations, will temporarily assume Somers’ responsibilities and serve as Interim head of operations.

data.title: Yunhong Green to effect 1-for-10 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Yunhong Green CTI (NASDAQ:YHGJ) will proceed with a 1-for-10 reverse stock split of its shares of common stock, the company said on Friday.
  • The reverse stock split is expected to be effective on October 1, 2025.
  • The split will reduce the number of shares outstanding from about 27.78M to approximately 2.77M.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: JFB Construction secures $43.9M PIPE investment from American ventures
data.description:
• JFB Construction Holdings (NASDAQ:JFB) entered a PIPE financing deal with American Ventures, raising ~$43.9M in gross proceeds.
  • The deal includes the sale of 4.29M Series C Convertible preferred stock at $10/share, convertible into 8.07M common shares.
  • Each preferred share unit includes two warrants- common warrants A: 8.07M warrants, exercisable at $5.75/share, expiring in 3 years and common warrants B: 8.07M warrants, exercisable at $6.25/share, also expiring in 3 years.
  • $12M of the net proceeds will be used to redeem Class B stock held by CEO Joseph F. Basile III.
  • Remaining funds will support general corporate operating expenses.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: British American Tobacco snaps six-day losing streak
data.description:
British American Tobacco (NYSE:BTI) snapped a six-day losing streak as its shares ended 1.24% higher at $53 on Friday.

The stock entered the red territory on September 18 when it ended 1.98% lower at $54.92. Since then, BTI has lost nearly 5%. On a year-to-date basis, however, BTI has gained over 45%, while in the last year it has advanced 39.5%.

Earlier this month, Argus upgraded BTI to Buy from Hold on the back of its pivot towards smokeless products over the past few years, which analyst Christine Dooley believes is now gaining traction.

As per Seeking Alpha’s quant ratings, it is recommended to Hold the stock. It has a score of 3.29 out of 5, with an A+ grade for profitability but a D for growth. However, Seeking Alpha and Wall Street analysts have a Buy call for the stock.

On Thursday, three Seeking Alpha analysts downgraded BTO to Hold while one maintained his Buy call.

Mike Zaccardi issued a Hold call, arguing that technicals suggest the stock’s potential pullback to the $48-$49 range, which he argued could offer a compelling entry point.

“Twelve months ago, I had a 'Buy' rating on British American Tobacco. Shares have returned 46% since then (49% YTD), leading me to downgrade the stock to a "Hold." The valuation is not extreme, but it’s now materially above its long-term average. The technicals suggest the chance of a further retreat off the mid-year high, but I’ll note a favorable entry point at the conclusion of the article,” he said.

Meanwhile, Steven Fiorillo said that BTI “remains undervalued, trading at under 12x 2025 earnings and yielding 5.79%, with strong free cash flow generation.”

data.title: Trump administration discussing tariffs on electronics based on number of chips: report
data.description:
• The Trump administration is discussing putting tariffs on foreign-made electronics based on the number of semiconductors they have inside of them, Reuters reported.
  • The U.S. Commerce Dept. could impose a tariff on the product that is equal to the percentage of the estimated value of the processors inside them, the news outlet added, citing people familiar with the matter. The news outlet added that the plan could still change.
  • All kinds of devices could be impacted, including electronic toothbrushes, laptops, phones and more, the news outlet added. 
  • The U.S. Commerce Dept. did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
  • Related tickers: Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL), HP (NYSE:HPQ), Dell (NYSE:DELL), Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF)
  • Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion

data.title: Eli Lilly upgraded at CFRA as minimal impact seen from pharma tariffs
data.description:
• CFRA Research upgraded Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) to buy from hold as it sees the drugmaker largely exempted from the 100% tariffs on imported branded medications. 
  • President Trump said that companies that are building new production facilities in the US will be free from the tariffs. 
  • The Indianapolis-based pharma announced in February it was doubling a prior commitment to boost US manufacturing to ~$50B worth of new investments. In September, Lilly said it would build a $5B production plant in Virginia. 
  • CFRA raised its price target to $835 from $700 (~15% upside based on Friday's close).
  • Analyst Sel Hardy said that Lilly is "well positioned with its plans to double down its investment footprint in the U.S., increasing its domestic drug production capacity across different therapy areas."
  • He added that earlier this week, Lilly said it would spend $6.5B on a new plant to manufacture small molecule drugs. 
  • Hardy also highlighted the recent approval of Alzheimer's therapy Kisunla (donanemab) in Europe as a positive.
